There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\n        # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\n        self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        self.nn_modules.clear()\n        self.param_name_to_source = None\n\n        for node in self.graph.nodes:\n            if \"grapharg\" in node.meta:\n                del node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n        self.real_value_cache.clear()\n        self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\n        self.side_effects.clear()\n        self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\n        self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\n        self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\n        self.tracing_context.clear()\n        self.input_source_to_var.clear()\n        self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\n        self.backward_state.clear()\n\n    def cleanup_on_exception(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Cleanup graphs from failed dynamo tracer outputs.\n        \n        These graphs hold onto reference cycles:\n        1. The nodes of the graph form a doubly linked list and point back to the graph\n        2. FakeTensorMode -> ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor -> FakeTensorMode\n        \n        To avoid needing a full garbage collection, manually clean up these reference\n        cycles to allow for immediate garbage collection.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Break the doubly linked list of graph nodes\n        for node in list(self.graph.nodes):\n            node.all_input_nodes.clear()\n        \n        # Clear TrackedFake references\n        self.tracked_fakes.clear()\n        \n        # Clear the ShapeEnv references\n        if self.shape_env is not None:\n            self.shape_env.cleanup()\n            # Clear the fake_tensor_cache which may hold references to FakeTensors\n            self.shape_env.fake_tensor_cache.clear()\n\n    def add_graph_finalizer("},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"cache_creation_input_tokens":808,"cache_read_input_tokens":94850,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":808},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"03073c36-4959-4319-ad5e-fbc5f878e6b4","uuid":"48f87241-cd4f-4d61-8d5c-321896fa8af5"}

This signals the\\n239:     autograd.grad boundary.\\n240: \\n241:     Args:\\n242:         tensors_with_sources: List of (tensor, source_name) tuples to start search from.\\n243:         stop_at: Optional set of grad_fns where traversal should stop (excluded from result).\\n244: \\n245:     Returns:\\n246:         Set of all reachable grad_fns.\\n247:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n248:     if stop_at is None:\\n249:         stop_at = set()\\n250: \\n251:     visited: set[torch.autograd.graph.Node] = set()\\n252:     stack: list[torch.autograd.graph.Node] = []\\n253: \\n254:     for tensor, _ in tensors_with_sources:\\n255:         if isinstance(tensor, torch.Tensor):\\n256:             grad_fn = tensor.grad_fn\\n257:             if grad_fn is not None:\\n258:                 stack.append(grad_fn)\\n259: \\n260:     while stack:\\n261:         node = stack.pop()\\n262:         if node in visited:\\n263:             continue\\n264:         # Stop traversal at stop_at nodes and don't include them\\n265:         # in consumed grad_fn list.\\n266:         if node in stop_at:\\n267:             continue\\n268:         visited.add(node)\\n269:         for next_fn, _ in node.next_functions:\\n270:             if next_fn is not None:\\n271:                 stack.append(next_fn)\\n272:     return visited\\n273: \\n274: \\n275: @functools.cache\\n276: def _step_logger() -> Any:\\n277:     return torchdynamo_logging.get_step_logger(log)\\n278: \\n279: \\n280: @dataclass\\n281: class GraphCompileReason:\\n282:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Stores why a given output graph was compiled; i.e. what caused the graph break.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n283: \\n284:     reason: str\\n285:     user_stack: list[traceback.FrameSummary]\\n286: \\n287:     # Each field\\n361:     is an int accumulator that gets bumped via ``time.time_ns()`` in the\\n362:     corresponding hot-path wrapper.  To add a new timer, add a field here\\n363:     and wire up the wrapper in the relevant function.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n364: \\n365:     get_fake_value_ns: int = 0\\n366:     create_proxy_ns: int = 0\\n367:     wrap_to_fake_tensor_and_record_ns: int = 0\\n368:     variable_builder_call_ns: int = 0\\n369: \\n370:     def report_and_reset(self) -> None:\\n371:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Flush accumulated timings to the bytecode_tracing chromium event\\n372:         and to compilation_time_metrics, then reset all counters.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n373:         chromium_log = get_chromium_event_logger()\\n374:         # pyrefly: ignore [implicit-any]\\n375:         event_data = {}\\n376:         for f in dataclasses.fields(self):\\n377:             ns_val = getattr(self, f.name)\\n378:             if ns_val > 0:\\n379:                 key = f.name.removesuffix(\\\"_ns\\\")\\n380:                 event_data[f\\\"{key}_time_s\\\"] = ns_val / 1e9\\n381:                 compilation_time_metrics.setdefault(key, []).append(ns_val / 1e9)\\n382:                 setattr(self, f.name, 0)\\n383:         if event_data:\\n384:             chromium_log.try_add_event_data(\\\"bytecode_tracing\\\", **event_data)\\n385: \\n386: \\n387: @dataclass\\n388: class OutputGraphGuardsState:\\n389:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n390:     A base class containing fields that are considered \\\"persistent\\\" when we\\n391:     want to save all the important state for reconstrucing guards in a different\\n392:     process. This is an\\n494:     # implementation detail -\\n495:     # https://docs.python.org/3/library/builtins.html.\\n496: \\n497:     # This makes guarding on any builtin messy because the guard check_fn\\n498:     # has to check if the __builtins__ is a module or dict, and then access\\n499:     # by either using getattr or getitem respectively.\\n500: \\n501:     # To solve this problem, we insert a new entry in f_globals which points\\n502:     # to the builtins __dict__ and then we guard any builtin on this dict.\\n503:     # To avoid any collision with the pre-existing keys, we use the\\n504:     # install_global to give us a unique dict key.\\n505: \\n506:     f_builtins = global_scope[\\\"__builtins__\\\"]\\n507:     if not isinstance(f_builtins, dict):\\n508:         f_builtins = f_builtins.__dict__\\n509:     return f_builtins\\n510: \\n511: \\n512: class OutputGraphCommon(OutputGraphGuardsState):\\n513:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n514:     A minimal interface for full graph capture. It is intended to be\\n515:     the target of any tracer that feeds into backends.\\n516: \\n517:     Currently dynamo's OutputGraph is the only known implementation\\n518:     of this interface, used by (aot) precompile and (strict) export.\\n519:     Importantly, that implementation also contains many other fields\\n520:     that are using during tracing but not included in this interface\\n521: type:ignore[assignment]\\n1194:             functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_dtype, \\\"cpu\\\"),\\n1195:             torch.get_autocast_dtype(\\\"cpu\\\"),\\n1196:         )\\n1197:         global_state[\\\"autocast_cache_enabled\\\"] = (\\n1198:             torch.set_autocast_cache_enabled,\\n1199:             torch.is_autocast_cache_enabled(),\\n1200:         )\\n1201: \\n1202:     def push_tx(self, tx: \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\") -> None:\\n1203:         self._current_tx.append(tx)\\n1204: \\n1205:     def pop_tx(self) -> \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\":\\n1206:         return self._current_tx.pop()\\n1207: \\n1208:     @property\\n1209:     def current_tx(self) -> \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\":\\n1210:         return self.root_tx if not self._current_tx else self._current_tx[-1]\\n1211: \\n1212:     def resolve_source_value(self, source: Source) -> Any:\\n1213:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1214:         Resolve the runtime value a Source points to using root_tx's frame.\\n1215: \\n1216:         Useful to inspect the python object associated with the source during\\n1217:         debugging. Will also be used by invoke subgraph caching later on.\\n1218:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1219:         return source.get_value(\\n1220:             {\\\"G\\\": self.root_tx.f_globals, \\\"L\\\": self.root_tx.f_locals},\\n1221:             {},\\n1222:             {},  # type: ignore[arg-type]\\n1223:         )\\n1224: \\n1225:     def count_calls(self) -> int:\\n1226:         return count_calls(self.graph)\\n1227: \\n1228:     def is_empty_graph(self) -> bool:\\n1229:         return len(list(self.graph.nodes)) == 0\\n1230: \\n1231:     def has_outputs(self) -> bool:\\n1232:         return len([x for x in self.graph.nodes if x.op == \\\"output\\\"]) > 0\\n1233: \\n1234:     def get_submodule(self, keys: str) -> Union[torch.nn.Module, Any]:\\n1235:         assert keys\\n1236:         obj: Union[torch.nn.Module, dict[str, torch.nn.Module]] = self.nn_modules\\n1237:         for k in keys.split(\\\".\\\"):\\n1238:             if isinstance(obj, dict):\\n1239:                 obj = obj[k]\\n1240:             else:\\n1241:                 obj = getattr(obj, k)\\n1242:         return obj\\n1243: \\n1244:     def new_var(self, name: str = \\\"tmp\\\") -> str:\\n1245:         existing = set(self.code_options[\\\"co_varnames\\\"])\\n1246:         # In common case, this will be O(1)\\n1247:         while True:\\n1248:             var = f\\\"{name}_{next(self.unique_var_id)}\\\"\\n1249:             if var not in existing:\\n1250:                 self.code_options[\\\"co_varnames\\\"] += (var,)\\n1251:                 return var\\n1252: \\n1253:     def update_co_names(self, name: str) -> None:\\n1254:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Ensure self.code_options.co_names contains name\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1255:         if name not in self.code_options[\\\"co_names\\\"]:\\n1256:             self.code_options[\\\"co_names\\\"] += (name,)\\n1257: \\n1258:     @staticmethod\\n1259:     def module_key_name(*names: Any) -> str:\\n1260:         # create a new unique name\\n1261:         name = \\\"_\\\".join(map(str, names))\\n1262:         # Strip _buffers[..]/_parameters[..]/_modules[..] names\\n1263:         name = re.sub(\\n1264:             r\\\"\\\\._(?:modules|parameters|buffers)\\\\[(['\\\\\\\"])([^'\\\\\\\"\\\\]]+)\\\\1\\\\]\\\", r\\\".\\\\2\\\", name\\n1265:         )\\n1266:         # Replace getattr(a, b) with a.b\\n1267:         name = re.sub(\\n1268:             r\\\"getattr\\\\(\\\\s*([^,]+?)\\\\s*,\\\\s*(['\\\\\\\"])([^'\\\\\\\"]+)\\\\2\\\\s*\\\\)\\\", r\\\"\\\\1.\\\\3\\\", name\\n1269:         )\\n1270:         # Strip the guard lookup L/G access\\n1271:         name = re.sub(r\\\"^[GL]\\\\['?(.*?)'?\\\\]$\\\", r\\\"\\\\1\\\", name)\\n1272:         # e.g. replace abc.xyz[123].qkv with abc.xyz_123.qkv\\n1273:         name = re.sub(r\\\"\\\\[(\\\\d+)\\\\]\\\", r\\\"_\\\\g<1>\\\", name)\\n1274:         # e.g. replace abc.xyz_123.qkv with abc_xyz_123_qkv\\n1275:         name = re.sub(r\\\"[^a-zA-Z0-9]\\\", \\\"_\\\", name)\\n1276: \\n1277:         if not name or not name[0].isalpha():\\n1278:             name = \\\"sub\\\" + name\\n1279: \\n1280:         return name\\n1281: \\n1282:     def register_static_attr_and_return_proxy(\\n1283:         self, attr_prefix: str, attr_value: Any\\n1284:     ) -> fx.Proxy:\\n1285:         # Instead, we want to raise the params/buffers\\n1322:                 # as inputs to the higher-order graph, and register them as\\n1323:                 # get_attrs in the root tracer.\\n1324: \\n1325:                 # Note that Dynamo will still call lift_tracked_freevar_to_input\\n1326:                 # when these inputs are encountered for the inner graph. The\\n1327:                 # only difference is what happens at the root tracer for\\n1328:                 # nn.Parameters vs free inputs. The free inputs are registered\\n1329:                 # as placeholders in the root graph, whereas the nn.Parameters\\n1330:                 # are registered as get_attr nodes in the root graph.\\n1331:                 tracer = self.root_tracer\\n1332: \\n1333:             def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\\n1334:                 assert self.param_name_to_source is not None\\n1335:                 self.param_name_to_source[module_key] = source\\n1336: \\n1337:                 # Check if the attr has already been registered. This can happen\\n1338:                 # when two different sources point to the same tensor.\\n1339:                 assert self.root_tx is not None\\n1340:                 if target in self.root_tx.output.side_effects:\\n1341:                     return self.root_tx.output.side_effects[target]\\n1342: \\n1343:                 if get_static_address_type(target) == \\\"guarded\\\" and not isinstance(\\n1344:                     source, NumpyTensorSource\\n1345:                 ):\\n1346:                     install_guard(source.make_guard(GuardBuilder.ID_MATCH))\\n1347:                 elif not is_constant_source(source):\\n1348:                     install_guard(source.make_guard(GuardBuilder.TENSOR_MATCH))\\n1349: \\n1350:                 vt = wrap_fx_proxy(\\n1351:                     self.root_tx,\\n1352:                     tracer.create_proxy(\\\"get_attr\\\", module_key, (), {}),\\n1353:                     example_value=target,\\n1354:                     **options,\\n1355:                 )\\n1356: \\n1357:                 # from higher order ops. NNModuleVariable tracker can't be\\n1380:                 # sourceless, so let's return a unspecializedNNModule variable\\n1381:                 # tracker.\\n1382:                 def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\\n1383:                     # pyrefly: ignore[bad-argument-type]\\n1384:                     return variables.UnspecializedNNModuleVariable(target, **options)\\n1385: \\n1386:         elif isinstance(target, (torch.SymInt, torch.SymFloat)):\\n1387:             # HACKY CODE REGION BEGIN\\n1388:             # WE ARE PIGGYBACKING ON EXISTING INFRA TO REGISTER ATTRS\\n1389:             # This ultimately gets written to self.nn_modules, which is unfortunate\\n1390:             # Attrs that are tenors and symints and such need to be migrated to have their\\n1391:             # own storage\\n1392:             # alas, this is like this for now\\n1393: \\n1394:             def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\\n1395:                 return SymNodeVariable.create(\\n1396:                     self.root_tx,\\n1397:                     self.create_proxy(\\\"get_attr\\\", module_key, (), {}),\\n1398:                     sym_num=target,\\n1399:                     **options,\\n1400:                 )\\n1401: \\n1402:             # HACKY CODE REGION END\\n1403:         else:\\n1404: \\n1405:             def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\\n1406:                 self.output.update_co_names(module_key)\\n1407:                 self.global_scope[module_key] = target\\n1408:                 return VariableTracker.build(\\n1409:                     self,  # it already exists\\n1417:                 return wrap_name(k)\\n1418: \\n1419:         name = OutputGraph.module_key_name(*names)\\n1420:         name = get_unique_name_wrt(name, self.nn_modules, self.global_scope)\\n1421:         self.nn_modules[name] = target\\n1422:         if isinstance(target, torch.nn.Module):\\n1423: \\n1424:             def register_leaf_name(leaf_name: str) -> None:\\n1425:                 assert self.param_name_to_source is not None\\n1426:                 new_source = self.get_chained_param_buffer_source(source, leaf_name)\\n1427:                 new_name = f\\\"{name}.{leaf_name}\\\"\\n1428:                 self.param_name_to_source[new_name] = new_source\\n1429:                 if isinstance(source, LocalSource):\\n1430:                     self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn[\\n1431:                         OutputGraph.module_key_name(new_source.name)\\n1432:                     ] = leaf_name\\n1433: \\n1434:             # If list inputs are stolen, but still needed after the function call, create aliases to keep them alive\\n1449:         maybe_gm = self.local_scope.get(\\\"self\\\")\\n1450:         stolen_list_names = get_locals_to_steal(maybe_gm)\\n1451:         if not stolen_list_names:\\n1452:             return [], {}\\n1453: \\n1454:         alias_insts = []\\n1455:         needs_alias: dict[str, list[VariableTracker]] = {}\\n1456: \\n1457:         queue = [\\n1458:             *tx.stack,\\n1459:             *tx.symbolic_locals.values(),\\n1460:             *self.side_effects.store_attr_mutations.keys(),\\n1461:         ]\\n1462: \\n1463:         while queue:\\n1464:             x = queue.pop()\\n1465:             if isinstance(x, BaseListVariable):\\n1466:                 assert isinstance(x.items, list)\\n1467:                 queue += x.items\\n1468:                 continue\\n1469: \\n1470:             if not (\\n1471:                 (\\n1472:                     x not in self.side_effects.store_attr_mutations\\n1473:                     or isinstance(x.mutation_type, AttributeMutationExisting)\\n1474:                 )\\n1475:                 and isinstance(x.source, GetItemSource)\\n1476:                 and isinstance(x.source.base, LocalSource)\\n1477:                 and x.source.base.local_name in stolen_list_names\\n1478:             ):\\n1479:                 continue\\n1480: \\n1481:             stolen_name = x.source.base.local_name\\n1482:             if stolen_name not in needs_alias:\\n1483:                 needs_alias[stolen_name] = []\\n1484:             needs_alias[stolen_name].append(x)\\n1485: \\n1486:         # pyrefly: ignore [implicit-any]\\n1487:         visited = {}\\n1488:         overridden_sources: dict[Source, Source] = {}\\n1489:         for arg in self.graphargs:\\n1490:             if not (\\n1491:                 isinstance(arg._example, list)\\n1492:                 and isinstance(arg.source, LocalSource)\\n1493:                 and arg.source.local_name in needs_alias\\n1494:             ):\\n1495:                 continue\\n1496: \\n1497:             # arg is a list that will be cleared by the compiled function\\n1498:             list_name = arg.source.local_name\\n1499:             assert list_name in self.code_options[\\\"co_varnames\\\"]\\n1500:             for x in needs_alias[list_name]:\\n1501:                 # Skip if already handled.\\n1502:                 if x.source in overridden_sources:\\n1503:                     continue\\n1504: \\n1505:                 # A small codegen optimization because we might have different\\n1506:                 # VariableTrackers that share the same source.\\n1507:                 assert x.source is not None\\n1508:                 list_idx = x.source.index  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n1509:                 if list_idx not in visited:\\n1510:                     alias_name = self.new_var(\\n1511:                         f\\\"{list_name}_ref\\\"\\n1512:                     )  # self.new_var already adds unique id suffix\\n1513: \\n1514:                     visited[list_idx] = alias_name\\n1515:                     # bytecode of `alias_name = list_name[list_idx]`\\n1516:                     alias_insts.extend(\\n1517:                         [\\n1518:                             create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=list_name),\\n1519:                             create_load_const(list_idx),\\n1520:                             create_binary_subscr(),\\n1521:                             create_instruction(\\\"STORE_FAST\\\", argval=alias_name),\\n1522:                         ]\\n1523:                     )\\n1524: \\n1525:                 # operate on alias, handled by suffix codegen\\n1526:                 assert x.source is not None\\n1527:                 old_source = x.source\\n1528:                 overridden_sources[old_source] = LocalSource(visited[list_idx])\\n1529: \\n1530:         # NOTE: we need `overridden_sources` because (1) we want to codegen for\\n1531:         # these list items to use the new local source, but (2) we want to avoid\\n1532:         # updating `source` in place because that might break invariants in\\n1533:         # other parts of Dynamo like guards.\\n1534:         return alias_insts, overridden_sources\\n1535: \\n1536:     def _get_stack_values_to_restore(\\n1537:         self, tx: \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\", stack_pops: int\\n1538:     ) -> tuple[list[VariableTracker], StackLocalsMetadata]:\\n1539:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1540:         Gets the stack + locals values belonging to tx that need to be restored.\\n1541: \\n1542:         Also prunes dead tx locals and realizes all VTs in the tx's stack.\\n1543: \\n1544:         NullVariables in stack/locals will NOT be restored, unless they are the top `stack_pops`\\n1545:         elements of the stack - it is expected that the next instruction to run will pop the top\\n1546:         `stack_pops` elements of the stack, so we should codegen NULLs.\\n1547: \\n1548:         Returns:\\n1549:             - stack_values: stack and locals values that need to be restored\\n1550:             - meta: locations of NULLs and ContextWrappingVariables in the stack/locals\\n1551:                 (ignores the top `stack_pops` values on the stack)\\n1552:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1553:         tx.prune_dead_locals()\\n1554: \\n1555:         stack_values = []\\n1556:         meta = StackLocalsMetadata()\\n1557: \\n1558:         def ctx_exit_check(var: VariableTracker) -> None:\\n1559:             if type.__instancecheck__(variables.WithExitFunctionVariable, var):\\n1560:                 raise AssertionError(\\n1561:                     \\\"Attempted to reconstruct WithExitFunctionVariable outside the stack\\\"\\n1562:                 )\\n1563: \\n1564:         # realize any unrealized tensor VTs in case they\\n1565:         # need to be added to self.nn_modules as attributes\\n1566:         for i, value in enumerate(tx.stack):\\n1567:             # Allow lazy constants through for values being returned (top of stack)\\n1568:             allow_lazy_constant = len(tx.stack) - i <= stack_pops\\n1569:             variables.LazyVariableTracker.realize_all(\\n1570:                 value, allow_lazy_constant=allow_lazy_constant\\n1571:             )\\n1572:             # Do not allow non-stack WithExitFunctionVariable reconstructions\\n1573:             if not isinstance(value, variables.WithExitFunctionVariable):\\n1574:                 VariableTracker.visit(ctx_exit_check, value)\\n1575:             # ignore top `stack_pops` values on the stack\\n1576:             if allow_lazy_constant:\\n1577:                 stack_values.append(value)\\n1578:                 continue\\n1579:             if isinstance(value, NullVariable):\\n1580:                 meta.stack_null_idxes.append(i)\\n1581:             else:\\n1582:                 stack_values.append(value)\\n1583:             if isinstance(value, ContextWrappingVariable):\\n1584:                 target_values = (\\n1585:                     () if value.target_values is None else tuple(value.target_values)\\n1586:                 )\\n1587:                 # NOTE: track index in stack after NULLs have been removed\\n1588:                 meta.stack_ctx_args.append((len(stack_values) - 1, target_values))\\n1589:                 meta.stack_ctx_idxes_orig.append(i)\\n1590: \\n1591:         meta.num_stack = len(stack_values)\\n1592: \\n1593:         cell_and_freevars = set(tx.cellvars() + tx.freevars())\\n1594: \\n1595:         # NB: Typically (i.e., for graph compile from RETURN_VALUE),\\n1596:         # symbolic_locals will be empty at this point, as prune_dead_locals\\n1597:         # will clear out all of symbolic_locals because RETURN_VALUE is the\\n1598:         # last instruction and no more locals are used.  The fanciness here\\n1599:         # is only needed for partial graphs.\\n1600:         # NOTE: All cell and free variables are represented as CellVariable,\\n1601:         # so checks for NULLs and context managers in the case of codegen'ing resume\\n1602:         # functions will not be performed on them. This is expected behavior.\\n1603:         for k, v in tx.symbolic_locals.items():\\n1604:             # Do not reconstruct WithExitFunctionVariable!\\n1605:             VariableTracker.visit(ctx_exit_check, v)\\n1606:             # Note! this explicitly uses .local_name for matching\\n1607:             # Failure to do so will cause spurious registrations in val_to_names.\\n1608:             # This will in turn result in spurious variables showing up in the graph.\\n1609:             # This was very tricky to debug. For an example, dump the graph at call_user_compiler\\n1610:             # while running test_subgraphs.py\\n1611:             # Do not include top-frame unmodified locals here - otherwise, the compiled graph may\\n1612:             # erroneously include them as part of the return. We manually codegen them afterward.\\n1613:             if (\\n1614:                 isinstance(v.source, LocalSource)\\n1615:                 and v.source.local_name == k\\n1616:                 and tx is self.root_tx\\n1617:             ):\\n1618:                 continue\\n1619:             # Do not load cell/free vars\\n1620:             if k in cell_and_freevars:\\n1621:                 continue\\n1622:             # Do not load variable if it is NULL.\\n1623:             if sys.version_info >= (3, 12):\\n1624:                 # NOTE: do not use isinstance, since it realizes lazy VT's\\n1625:                 # Continuation function will load the NULL for v.\\n1626:                 if type.__instancecheck__(NullVariable, v):\\n1627:                     meta.locals_null_keys.append(k)\\n1628:                     continue\\n1629:             else:\\n1630:                 # A variable should never be NULL in < 3.12\\n1631:                 assert not type.__instancecheck__(NullVariable, v)\\n1632:             meta.locals_names[k] = len(meta.locals_names)\\n1633:             if isinstance(v, ContextWrappingVariable):\\n1634:                 target_values = (\\n1635:                     () if v.target_values is None else tuple(v.target_values)\\n1636:                 )\\n1637:                 meta.locals_ctx_args.append((k, target_values))\\n1638:             stack_values.append(v)\\n1639: \\n1640:         return stack_values, meta\\n1641: \\n1642:     def compile_subgraph(\\n1643:         self,\\n1644:         tx: \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\",\\n1645:         reason: GraphCompileReason,\\n1646:         stack_pops: int = 0,\\n1647:     ) -> list[StackLocalsMetadata]:\\n1648:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1649:         Compiles the current subgraph, with inputs w.r.t. self.root_tx, and codegens:\\n1650:             - Call the compiled subgraph\\n1651:             - Apply side effects\\n1652:             - Codegen stack and locals\\n1653:             - Store the locals\\n1654: \\n1655:         Python does not allow NULL to be an arg to a function, so we do not codegen NULLs on the stack,\\n1656:         unless the value is one of the top `stack_pops` values on the stack (these values are expected to be\\n1657:         popped immediately after this generated code. The prologue of the resume function is expected to restore\\n1658:         any dropped NULLs.\\n1659: \\n1660:         Returns stack indices and locals keys where we dropped NULLs, and where we found inactive context manager objects.\\n1661:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1662: \\n1663:         assert self.root_tx is not None\\n1664: \\n1665:         if not config.nested_graph_breaks:\\n1666:             # expect to only compile 1 frame\\n1667:             assert self.root_tx is tx\\n1668: \\n1669:         # bytecode tracing has finished. Pop the context manager for dynamo_timed\\n1670:         self.mark_bytecode_tracing_stop()\\n1671: \\n1672:         self.compile_subgraph_reason = reason\\n1673:         self.should_exit = True\\n1674: \\n1675:         log.debug(\\\"COMPILING GRAPH due to %s\\\", reason)\\n1676: \\n1677:         # The nodes of the graph form a doubly linked list and point back to the graph\n        2. FakeTensorMode → ShapeEnv → TrackedFake → FakeTensor → FakeTensorMode\n\n        To avoid needing a full garbage collection, manually clean up these reference\n        cycles to allow for immediate garbage collection.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Break the doubly linked list of graph nodes by clearing node references\n        for node in list(self.graph.nodes):\n            # Clear all input references from the node\n            node.all_input_nodes.clear()\n\n        # Clear the tracked_fakes list to release references to fake tensors\n        # This breaks the cycle: ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor\n        self.tracked_fakes.clear()\n\n        # Call cleanup on the ShapeEnv to clean up symbolic shape information\n        # and break any remaining cycles\n        self.shape_env.cleanup()\n\n        # Clear the fake_tensor_cache to release references to cached fake tensors\n        self.fake_mode.fake_tensor_cache.clear()"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"cache_creation_input_tokens":740,"cache_read_input_tokens":117298,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":740},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"03073c36-4959-4319-ad5e-fbc5f878e6b4","uuid":"e1a0c9d9-f74e-4349-b669-0f623af81aec"}

stop_at: set[torch.autograd.graph.Node] | None = None,\n) -> set[torch.autograd.graph.Node]:\n    \"\"\"Collect all grad_fns reachable from tensors' autograd graphs.\n\n    Performs a DFS traversal and collects all visited grad_fns.\n    Optionally stops traversal nodes in stop_at set. This signals the\n    autograd.grad boundary.\n\n    Args:\n        tensors_with_sources: List of (tensor, source_name) tuples to start search from.\n        stop_at: Optional set of grad_fns where traversal should stop (excluded from result).\n\n    Returns:\n        Set of all reachable grad_fns.\n    \"\"\"\n    if stop_at is None:\n        stop_at = set()\n\n    visited: set[torch.autograd.graph.Node] = set()\n    stack: list[torch.autograd.graph.Node] = []\n\n    for tensor, _ in tensors_with_sources:\n        if isinstance(tensor, torch.Tensor):\n            grad_fn = tensor.grad_fn\n            if grad_fn is not None:\n                stack.append(grad_fn)\n\n    while stack:\n        node = stack.pop()\n        if node in visited:\n            continue\n        # Stop traversal at stop_at nodes and don't include them\n        # in consumed grad_fn list.\n        if node in stop_at:\n            continue\n        visited.add(node)\n        for next_fn, _ in node.next_functions:\n            if next_fn is not None:\n                stack.append(next_fn)\n    return visited\n\n\n@functools.cache\ndef _step_logger() -> Any:\n    return torchdynamo_logging.get_step_logger(log)\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass GraphCompileReason:\n    \"\"\"Stores why a given output graph was compiled; i.e. what caused the graph break.\"\"\"\n\n    reason: str\n    user_stack: list[traceback.FrameSummary]\n\n    # This is an\n    # implementation detail -\n    # https://docs.python.org/3/library/builtins.html.\n\n    # This makes guarding on any builtin messy because the guard check_fn\n    # has to check if the __builtins__ is a module or dict, and then access\n    # by either using getattr or getitem respectively.\n\n    # To solve this problem, we insert a new entry in f_globals which points\n    # to the builtins __dict__ and then we guard any builtin on this dict.\n    # To avoid any collision with the pre-existing keys, we use the\n    # install_global to give us a unique dict key.\n\n    f_builtins = global_scope[\"__builtins__\"]\n    if not isinstance(f_builtins, dict):\n        f_builtins = f_builtins.__dict__\n    return f_builtins\n\n\nclass OutputGraphCommon(OutputGraphGuardsState):\n    \"\"\"\n    A minimal interface for full graph capture. It is intended to be\n    the target of any tracer that feeds into backends.\n\n    Currently dynamo's OutputGraph is the only known implementation\n    of this interface, used by (aot) precompile and (strict) export.\n    Importantly, that implementation also contains many other fields\n    that are using during tracing but not included in this interface\n    because they are not used once tracing is complete.\n\n    It should be safe to assume that (caching) precompile also uses\n    this interface.\n\n    In the future, we want make_fx, used by (non-strict) export, to\n    also implement this interface.\n\n    The serializable part of this interface is OutputGraphGuardsState.\n    We do not need to serialize other parts; however it will pay to\n    be disciplined about what those other parts are, especially since\n    we want other tracers to be able to meaningfully implement them,\n    and we should generally try to cut them down when possible.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n Mainly the\n    generated fx.Graph.\n\n    OutputGraph is 1:1 with a frame being processed. Each frame is associated\n    with some root InstructionTranslator. When user code calls a function,\n    we construct a InliningInstructionTranslator that continues to write into\n    the root InstructionTranslator's OutputGraph.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    side_effects: SideEffects\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        code_options: dict[str, Any],\n        compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn],\n        root_tx: \"InstructionTranslatorBase\",\n        export: bool,\n        export_constraints: Sequence[_ConstraintTarget],\n        frame_state: Any,\n        local_scope: Scope,\n        global_scope: Scope,\n        f_code: CodeType,\n        torch_function_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode],\n        package: Optional[\"CompilePackage\"],\n        one_graph: bool = False,\n    ) -> None:\n        OutputGraphGuardsState.__init__(\n            self,\n            local_scope,\n            global_scope,\n            torch_function_mode_stack,\n            guard_on_key_order=set(),\n            input_source_to_sizes_strides={},\n            dual_level=torch.autograd.forward_ad._current_level,\n            functorch_layers=torch._functorch.pyfunctorch.retrieve_all_functorch_interpreters(),\n            current_device=torch.utils._device.CURRENT_DEVICE,\n            # initial_global_state is only None during NopTest.\n            global_state_guard=torch._dynamo.convert_frame.initial_global_state\n            or torch._C._dynamo.guards.GlobalStateGuard(),\n            # These are set by @property instead, just initialize them as blank\n            _guards=torch._guards.GuardsSet(),\n            _aotautograd_guards=[],\n        )\n        self.tracers = [SubgraphTracer(self, is_export=export)]\n        # Map from graph input's `Source` to its `VariableTracker` to\n        # de-duplicate graph inputs by source and reuse the tracker\n        self.input_source_to_var: dict[Source, VariableTracker] = {}\n        # List of TensorVariables that are leaf tensors created in-graph\n        # (e.g., nn.Parameter via tracable_create_parameter). These need to be\n        # tracked separately from input_source_to_var for backward() auto-detection.\n        self.leaf_var_creation_order: list[VariableTracker] = []\n        self.export = export\n        self.export_constraints = export_constraints  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        self.frame_state = frame_state\n        self.cleanup_hooks: list[Callable[[], Any]] = []\n        # compile_id is an id number for the current torch.compile\n        self.compile_id: int = next(_compile_id_counter)\n        # Set of globals installed via install_global* APIs\n        self.installed_globals: set[str] = set()\n\n        # TODO: maybe should just pass the entire f_code in here?  Not\n        # sure...\n        self.co_fields = {\n            \"co_name\": f_code.co_name,\n            \"co_filename\": f_code.co_filename,\n            \"co_firstlineno\": f_code.co_firstlineno,\n        }\n\n        # Cudagraph annotation is set during inlining in\n        # InliningInstructionTranslator.build_inline_tracer when a decorated\n        # function is inlined.\n        self.cudagraph_annotation: Optional[_CudagraphAnnotation] = None\n\n        self.region_tracker = GraphRegionTracker()\n        self._emit_debugger_breakpoint: bool = False\n\n        # tracked_fakes says where any tensor that was wrapped to fake came\n        # from.  It is similar to GraphArg, in that all GraphArgs will get\n        # will get added to TrackedFakes, but TrackedFakes also contains\n        # GraphArgs that got pruned, and things like Tensor attributes which\n        # aren't explicit graph inputs.  Used by shape guard\n        self.tracked_fakes: list[TrackedFake] = []\n\n        shape_env = ShapeEnv(\n            # Reference Cycle!\n            # Share a reference to the list of TrackedFake.\n            #\n            # ShapeEnv needs this in order to be able to reproduce the call\n            # to produce_guards at an arbitrary time point. That is because\n            # TrackedFake instances may have its metadata changed throughout\n            # the program execution.\n            tracked_fakes=self.tracked_fakes,\n            # We want to allow capture scalar outputs and allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops when fullgraph=True\n            allow_scalar_outputs=one_graph or config.capture_scalar_outputs,\n            allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops=one_graph\n            or config.capture_dynamic_output_shape_ops,\n            prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards=config.prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards,\n            co_fields=self.co_fields,\n        )\n\n        # In export mode, we force the shape_env to strictly disallow any constraining\n        # of the user marked dynamic dims\n        import torch._functorch.config as _config\n\n        with _config.patch(fake_tensor_allow_unsafe_data_ptr_access=False):\n            fake_mode = torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode(\n                shape_env=shape_env,\n                # TODO (tmanlaibaatar) Remove this once we always lift params and buffers\n                allow_non_fake_inputs=bool(self.export),\n                export=self.export,\n            )\n        self.tracing_context: TracingContext = TracingContext(fake_mode)\n        self.tracing_context.traced_code.append(f_code)\n        self.tracing_context.cudagraph_annotation = self.cudagraph_annotation\n        self.traced_code = self.tracing_context.traced_code\n        self.dynamo_compile_id: Optional[CompileId] = (\n            CompileContext.current_compile_id()\n        )\n        self.init_ambient_guards()\n\n        # Map each tensor id to a list of sources. This is necessary because\n        # tensor ids cannot be recovered from tracked fakes (in general).\n        # We use this map to interpret (i.e., check for violations of) constraints,\n        # specifically equality constraints, which have shared tensor ids in them.\n        # This map should also be generally useful, e.g., for (de)serialization.\n        self.tracked_fakes_id_to_source: dict[int, list[Source]] = (\n            collections.defaultdict(list)\n        )\n        # Stores the full fqn of a param or buffer to the relevant source.\n        self.param_name_to_source: Optional[dict[str, Source]] = {}\n        self.side_effects = SideEffects(self)\n        # Cached variable trackers. This makes symbolic analysis of LOAD_GLOBAL\n        # and LOAD_ATTR for same python objects free.\n        self.variable_tracker_cache: dict[Source, VariableTracker] = {}\n        # Cache for sources resolved via MRO walk, keyed by id(obj).\n        # When the same descriptor (e.g. property) is reached from multiple\n        # subclasses, we reuse the first source to avoid redundant guards.\n        # We thought of rolling this in variable_tracker_cache but here\n        # different sources point to the same object, we also don't want it to\n        # go through the side effects cache because even though these objects\n        # are same, we dont want OBJECT_ALIASING guards on them. For these\n        # objects, we have DICT_CONTAINS absent guards on the mro walk, so there\n        # is no need of the OBJECT_ALIASING guards.\n        self.mro_source_cache: dict[tuple[int, str], DictGetItemSource] = {}\n        # Tracks (id(klass), attr_name) pairs that already have a\n        # DICT_CONTAINS absent guard installed during MRO walks.  When\n        # multiple subclasses share the same intermediate MRO class, we\n        # only need to guard the absence once per (class, attr) pair.\n        self.guarded_mro_absent_keys: set[tuple[int, str]] = set()\n        # Cache for inspect.signature results: function -> VariableTracker\n        self.signature_cache: dict[Any, VariableTracker] = {}\n        self.unique_var_id = itertools.count()\n        self.code_options: dict[str, Any] = dict(code_options)\n        self.output_instructions: list[Instruction] = []\n        # used to track nodes that are added between calls of copy_graphstate\n        # and restore_graphstate\n        self.timestamp = 0\n\n        # A list of register_finalizer_fns to apply to the output graph module\n        self.register_finalizer_fns: list[Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]] = []\n\n        # Not checkpointed\n        self.compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn] = compiler_fn\n        self.root_tx = root_tx\n\n        # Profiler state for tracking function trace timings\n        self.profiler_state: Optional[DynamoProfilerState] = None\n\n        self.package = package\n        # Given a source, what are the user stacks of all locations that\n        # accessed it?\n        #\n        # For efficiency, we only populate this:\n        #   - During export, and\n        #   - If the source could potentially lead to a spurious export input\n        #\n        # Feel free to populate this more frequently if other use-cases arise,\n        # but be aware that we have to generate full stacks for each\n        # recording!\n        self.source_to_user_stacks: dict[Source, list[traceback.StackSummary]] = {}\n\n        self._current_tx: list[InstructionTranslatorBase] = []\n        self.cleanups: list[CleanupHook] = []\n        self.should_exit = False\n        self.unspec_variable_map: dict[str, UnspecializedPythonVariable] = {}\n\n        # This returns false if TF Overall (both mode and subclass) is disabled OR that TF Mode stack is empty\n        self.torch_function_mode_enabled = torch._C._is_torch_function_mode_enabled()\n\n        # Tracks if the output graph has a user defined allowed function in the\n        # graph. This is used later to determine if we should fallback to eager\n        # for certain exceptions. THe idea is that if the user has applied\n        # allow_in_graph, they would like to see the error instead of falling\n        # back for backend errors.\n        self.has_user_defined_allowed_in_graph = False\n\n        # Tracks ALL grad_fn nodes that are consumed by torch.autograd.grad(outputs, inputs).\n        # This is the set of all nodes reachable from outputs' grad_fns, excluding inputs' grad_fns\n        # (since autograd.grad stops at inputs without consuming them).\n        # Used to detect returning tensors connected to consumed grad_fns (would cause\n        # \"backward through graph a second time\" error in aot_autograd).\n        self.autograd_grad_consumed_grad_fns: set[torch.autograd.graph.Node] = set()\n\n        # Tracks a list of called ops that were not tagged with \"pt2_compliant_tag\".\n        # This information is useful for logging.\n        self.non_compliant_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\n\n        # Tracks a list of called custom ops that were tagged with \"pt2_compliant_tag\".\n        # This information is useful for logging.\n        self.compliant_custom_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\n\n        # We save the global torch state here to be restored in case of graph\n        # breaks. The relevant issue is seen here\n        # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/100570#issuecomment-1543427086\n        # where inlining of a function changes the global state (because of the\n        # presence of torch.no_grad) and there is a graph break.\n        self.save_global_state()\n\n        # Tracks the original FQNs of the constant tensors from the original graph,\n        # i.e. buffers and parameters.\n        self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn: dict[str, str] = {}\n\n        # All calls to random() are replaced with a single call to __gen_rand_values\n        # functions that returns a tuple of random values for each original call.\n        # random_calls tracks calls to random() and random_values_var stores the name of\n        # the variable that stores __gen_rand_values results.\n        self.random_calls: list[\n            tuple[Callable[..., object], tuple[object, ...], dict[str, object]]\n        ] = []\n        self.random_values_var: Any = None\n\n        # Bytecode to insert right before we call the graph\n        self.pregraph_bytecode: list[Instruction] = []\n\n        # Use to pass values to backward hooks when using compiled autograd\n        self.backward_state: dict[str, VariableTracker] = {}\n        self.backward_state_proxy: Optional[torch.fx.Proxy] = None\n        self.backward_state_var: Optional[str] = None\n\n        # pyrefly: ignore [bad-override]\n        self.name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals: str = (\n            self.install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals()\n        )\n\n        self.compiler_trace_stack = contextlib.ExitStack()\n\n        self.bytecode_tracing_timings = BytecodeTracingTimings()\n\n        # These are the ambient, currently-global saved_tensor_hooks stashed in autograd,\n        # that are set for the entire duration of the compiled region.\n        # This is an invariant today because we graph break on the saved_tensor_hook\n        # context manager inside a compiled region\n        self.saved_tensors_hooks_subgraph_names: Optional[list[str]] = (\n            self.maybe_install_saved_tensors_hooks_subgraphs()\n        )\n\n        # mangled alias -> module fqn name\n        self.import_sources: dict[str, str] = {}\n\n        self.export_metadata = ExportMetaData()\n\n        # Set of inlined unspecialized modules names to generate the\n        # dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn mapping.\n        self.used_inlined_inbuilt_modules_names: OrderedSet[str] = OrderedSet()\n\n        self.attr_source_cache: dict[tuple[Source, str], AttrSource] = {}\n\n    def get_chained_attr_source(self, base: Source, path: str) -> AttrSource:\n        parts = path.split(\".\")\n        key = (base, parts[0])\n        if key not in self.attr_source_cache:\n            self.attr_source_cache[key] = AttrSource(base, parts[0])\n        result = self.attr_source_cache[key]\n        for part in parts[1:]:\n            key = (result, part)\n            if key not in self.attr_source_cache:\n                self.attr_source_cache[key] = AttrSource(result, part)\n            result = self.attr_source_cache[key]\n        return result\n\n    def get_chained_param_buffer_source(\n        self, base: Source, path: str\n    ) -> \"ParamBufferSource\":\n        parts = path.rsplit(\".\", 1)\n        if len(parts) == 1:\n            return ParamBufferSource(base, path)\n        intermediate_base = self.get_chained_attr_source(base, parts[0])\n        return ParamBufferSource(intermediate_base, parts[1])\n\n    def mark_bytecode_tracing_start(self) -> None:\n        self.compiler_trace_stack.enter_context(\n            dynamo_timed(\n                \"bytecode_tracing\",\n                log_pt2_compile_event=True,\n            )\n        )\n        # Start profiler timing for the root function\n        if config.dynamo_profiler:\n            from torch._dynamo.dynamo_profiler import DynamoProfilerState\n\n            if self.profiler_state is None:\n                self.profiler_state = DynamoProfilerState()\n            code = self.root_tx.f_code\n            self.profiler_state.push(\n                code.co_name,\n                code.co_filename,\n                code.co_firstlineno,\n                time.time_ns(),\n            )\n\n    def mark_bytecode_tracing_stop(self) -> None:\n        self.bytecode_tracing_timings.report_and_reset()\n        self.compiler_trace_stack.close()\n        # Record profiler timing for the root function and dump stats\n        if config.dynamo_profiler and self.profiler_state is not None:\n            from torch._dynamo.dynamo_profiler import FunctionTraceTiming\n\n            stack_entry = self.profiler_state.pop()\n            trace_end_ns = time.time_ns()\n            if stack_entry is not None:\n                cumtime_ns = trace_end_ns - stack_entry.start_time_ns\n                tottime_ns = cumtime_ns - stack_entry.child_time_ns\n                timing = FunctionTraceTiming(\n                    func_name=stack_entry.func_name,\n                    filename=stack_entry.filename,\n                    firstlineno=stack_entry.firstlineno,\n                    cumtime_ns=cumtime_ns,\n                    tottime_ns=tottime_ns,\n                    bytecode_count=len(self.root_tx.f_code.co_code),\n                    inline_depth=0,\n                    caller_func_name=None,\n                    caller_filename=None,\n                    caller_firstlineno=None,\n                    is_primitive_call=stack_entry.is_primitive_call,\n                    call_stack=(),\n                )\n                self.profiler_state.record_timing(timing)\n\n            # Dump profiler stats\n            output_file = None\n            if isinstance(config.dynamo_profiler, str):\n                output_file = config.dynamo_profiler\n            self.profiler_state.dump_stats(output_file)\n\n    def install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals(self) -> str:\n        f_builtins = get_builtins_dict(self.global_scope)\n        return self.install_global(\"__builtins_dict__\", f_builtins)\n\n    def add_backward_state_hook(\n        self, hook: VariableTracker, prefix: str = \"hook\"\n    ) -> tuple[str, torch.fx.Proxy]:\n        name = f\"{prefix}{len(self.backward_state)}\"\n        assert name not in self.backward_state\n        self.backward_state[name] = hook\n        return name, self.get_backward_state_proxy()\n\n    def get_backward_state_proxy(self) -> torch.fx.Proxy:\n        if self.backward_state_proxy is None:\n            if self.export:\n                unimplemented(\n                    gb_type=\"backward_state does not support export\",\n                    context=\"\",\n                    explanation=\"Compiled autograd doesn't work with `torch.export`.\",\n                    hints=[],\n                )\n            example_value = BackwardState()\n            self.backward_state_proxy = self.root_tracer.create_graph_input(\n                \"dynamo_backward_state\",\n                type(example_value),\n                example_value,\n                source=BackwardStateSource(),\n            )\n            self.backward_state_proxy.node.meta[\"grapharg\"] = BackwardStateGraphArg()\n            self.backward_state_var = self.new_var()\n        return self.backward_state_proxy\n\n    # This gets its own helper function so guards DEBUG logs are more informative\n    def init_ambient_guards(self) -> None:\n        # Register a SHAPE_ENV guard to make sure we setup shape guards\n        # that show up in ShapeEnv\n        self.guards.add(ShapeEnvSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.SHAPE_ENV))\n\n        self.guards.add(\n            GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.DETERMINISTIC_ALGORITHMS)\n        )\n\n        self.guards.add(GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.GRAD_MODE))\n\n        self.guards.add(GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.DEFAULT_DEVICE))\n\n        self.guards.add(GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.GLOBAL_STATE))\n        self.guards.add(\n            GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.TORCH_FUNCTION_STATE)\n        )\n\n        ci = torch._C._functorch.peek_interpreter_stack()\n        if ci is not None:\n            self.guards.add(\n                GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.FUNCTORCH_STACK_MATCH)\n            )\n        if not torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd.in_compiled_autograd_region:\n            self.guards.add(\n                GlobalStateSource().make_guard(\n                    GuardBuilder.AUTOGRAD_SAVED_TENSORS_HOOKS\n                )\n            )\n\n    def maybe_install_saved_tensors_hooks_subgraphs(self) -> Optional[list[str]]:\n        if torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd.in_compiled_autograd_region:\n            return None\n\n        get_hooks = torch._functorch._aot_autograd.utils.top_saved_tensors_hooks\n        are_inline_hooks = (\n            torch._functorch._aot_autograd.utils.saved_tensors_hooks_are_inlineable\n        )\n        hooks = get_hooks()\n        if not are_inline_hooks(hooks):\n            return None\n\n        # If GraphModule provided by user contains fx.wrap,\n        # We can only rely on user provided cache hash in this case.\n        # If user did not provide cache hash - then we always bypass cache.\n\n        pack_gm, unpack_gm = hooks\n        pack_subgraph_name = self.install_subgraph(\n            \"saved_tensors_hooks_pack\",\n            torch.fx.GraphModule(self.nn_modules, pack_gm.graph),\n        )\n        unpack_subgraph_name = self.install_subgraph(\n            \"saved_tensors_hooks_unpack\",\n            torch.fx.GraphModule(self.nn_modules, unpack_gm.graph),\n        )\n        assert pack_subgraph_name == \"saved_tensors_hooks_pack_0\"\n        assert unpack_subgraph_name == \"saved_tensors_hooks_unpack_0\"\n        return [pack_subgraph_name, unpack_subgraph_name]\n\n    def synthetic_graph_input(\n        self, fn: Callable[..., Any], args: tuple[Any, ...]\n    ) -> VariableTracker:\n        \"\"\"\n        call fn(*args) before the graph runs and turn the result into a fake input.\n        \"\"\"\n        example_value = fn(*args)\n        varname = self.new_var()\n        cg = PyCodegen(self.root_tx)\n        cg.add_push_null(\n            lambda: cg.load_import_from(\n                fn.__module__,\n                fn.__name__,\n            )\n        )\n        cg.foreach(map(variables.ConstantVariable.create, args))\n        cg.call_function(len(args), False)\n        cg.store(varname)\n        self.pregraph_bytecode.extend(cg.get_instructions())\n        source = SyntheticLocalSource(varname)\n        result = VariableTracker.build(self.root_tx, example_value, source)\n        # Realize the VT because we will delete the guards on it in the next line.\n        result = result.realize()\n        TracingContext.get().guards_context.dynamo_guards.remove_guards_with_source(\n            source\n        )\n        return result\n\n    def add_cleanup_hook(self, fn: Callable[[], Any]) -> None:\n        self.cleanup_hooks.append(fn)\n\n    def call_cleanup_hooks(self) -> None:\n        for hook in reversed(self.cleanup_hooks):\n            hook()\n        self.cleanup_hooks.clear()\n\n    @property\n    def root_tracer(self) -> \"SubgraphTracer\":\n        return self.tracers[0]\n\n    @property\n    def current_tracer(self) -> \"SubgraphTracer\":\n        return self.tracers[-1]\n\n    def is_root_tracer(self) -> bool:\n        # Helper to tell if we are inside the higher order operator tracing.\n        return len(self.tracers) == 1\n\n    @property\n    def graph(self) -> torch.fx.Graph:\n        return self.current_tracer.graph\n\n    # TODO(rzou): can delete after we refactor speculate_subgraph to use nested GraphTracer.\n    @graph.setter\n    def graph(self, value: torch.fx.Graph) -> None:\n        self.current_tracer.graph = value\n\n    @property\n    def input_name_to_proxy(self) -> dict[str, fx.Proxy]:\n        return self.current_tracer.input_name_to_proxy\n\n    @property\n    def real_value_cache(self) -> dict[fx.Node, torch.Tensor]:\n        return self.current_tracer.real_value_cache\n\n    @property\n    def bound_symbols(self) -> dict[sympy.Symbol, Union[torch.fx.Proxy, \"LazyProxy\"]]:\n        return self.current_tracer.bound_symbols\n\n    # If you are here, and you're looking for create_graph_input,\n    # to avoid ambiguity, please call one of the following:\n    # - self.current_tracer.create_graph_input\n    # - self.root_tracer.create_graph_input\n    # See NOTE [HigherOrderOperator tracing design] for more context.\n\n    def create_proxy(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> torch.fx.Proxy:\n        return self.current_tracer.create_proxy(*args, **kwargs)\n\n    def create_node(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> torch.fx.Node:\n        return self.current_tracer.create_node(*args, **kwargs)\n\n    def remove_node(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:\n        return self.current_tracer.remove_node(*args, **kwargs)\n\n    @contextlib.contextmanager\n    def subtracer(\n        self,\n        source_target: Optional[Target],\n        prior_tracer: Optional[\"SubgraphTracer\"],\n        description: Optional[str] = None,\n    ) -> Generator[\"SubgraphTracer\", None, None]:\n        new_scope_ctx = enter_new_scope()\n        try:\n            if prior_tracer:\n                # Lineage MUST stay preserved\n                assert prior_tracer.parent is self.current_tracer\n            new_scope_ctx.__enter__()\n            tracer = (\n                prior_tracer\n                if prior_tracer\n                else SubgraphTracer(\n                    self,\n                    parent=self.current_tracer,\n                    source_target=source_target,\n                    is_export=self.current_tracer.is_export,\n                    description=description,\n                )\n            )\n            self.tracers.append(tracer)\n            yield tracer\n        finally:\n            new_scope_ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n            self.tracers.pop()\n\n    @property\n    def output(self) -> \"OutputGraph\":\n        return self\n\n    @property\n    def fake_mode(self) -> torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode:\n        assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode is not None\n        return self.tracing_context.fake_mode\n\n    @property\n    def shape_env(self) -> ShapeEnv:\n        assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode is not None\n        assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode.shape_env is not None\n        return self.tracing_context.fake_mode.shape_env\n\n    @property\n    def guards(self) -> torch._guards.GuardsSet:\n        return self.tracing_context.guards_context.dynamo_guards\n\n    @property\n    def nn_modules(self) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        return self.tracing_context.module_context.nn_modules\n\n    @property\n    def aotautograd_guards(self) -> list[torch._guards.GuardEnvExpr]:\n        return self.tracing_context.guards_context.aotautograd_guards\n\n    def save_global_state(\n        self, out: Optional[dict[str, tuple[Callable[..., Any], bool]]] = None\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Saves to out if it is provided. Else saves to the tracing context's global_state.\n        \"\"\"\n        global_state = cast(\n            dict[str, tuple[Callable[..., Any], bool]],\n            (\n                out\n                if out is not None\n                else self.tracing_context.global_context.global_state\n            ),\n        )\n\n        global_state[\"grad_enabled\"] = (torch.set_grad_enabled, torch.is_grad_enabled())\n\n        global_state[\"autocast_enabled\"] = (\n            functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_enabled, \"cuda\"),\n            torch.is_autocast_enabled(\"cuda\"),\n        )\n        global_state[\"autocast_cpu_enabled\"] = (\n            functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_enabled, \"cpu\"),\n            torch.is_autocast_enabled(\"cpu\"),\n        )\n        global_state[\"autocast_gpu_dtype\"] = (  # type:ignore[assignment]\n            functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_dtype, \"cuda\"),\n            torch.get_autocast_dtype(\"cuda\"),\n        )\n        global_state[\"autocast_cpu_dtype\"] = (  # type:ignore[assignment]\n            functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_dtype, \"cpu\"),\n            torch.get_autocast_dtype(\"cpu\"),\n        )\n        global_state[\"autocast_cache_enabled\"] = (\n            torch.set_autocast_cache_enabled,\n            torch.is_autocast_cache_enabled(),\n        )\n\n    def push_tx(self, tx: \"InstructionTranslatorBase\") -> None:\n        self._current_tx.append(tx)\n\n    def pop_tx(self) -> \"InstructionTranslatorBase\":\n        return self._current_tx.pop()\n\n    @property\n    def current_tx(self) -> \"InstructionTranslatorBase\":\n        return self.root_tx if not self._current_tx else self._current_tx[-1]\n\n    def resolve_source_value(self, source: Source) -> Any:\n        \"\"\"\n        Resolve the runtime value a Source points to using root_tx's frame.\n\n        Useful to inspect the python object associated with the source during\n        debugging. Will also be used by invoke subgraph caching later on.\n        \"\"\"\n        return source.get_value(\n            {\"G\": self.root_tx.f_globals, \"L\": self.root_tx.f_locals},\n            {},\n            {},  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n        )\n\n    def count_calls(self) -> int:\n        return count_calls(self.graph)\n\n    def is_empty_graph(self) -> bool:\n        return len(list(self.graph.nodes)) == 0\n\n    def has_outputs(self) -> bool:\n        return len([x for x in self.graph.nodes if x.op == \"output\"]) > 0\n\n    def get_submodule(self, keys: str) -> Union[torch.nn.Module, Any]:\n        assert keys\n        obj: Union[torch.nn.Module, dict[str, torch.nn.Module]] = self.nn_modules\n        for k in keys.split(\".\"):\n            if isinstance(obj, dict):\n                obj = obj[k]\n            else:\n                obj = getattr(obj, k)\n        return obj\n\n    def new_var(self, name: str = \"tmp\") -> str:\n        existing = set(self.code_options[\"co_varnames\"])\n        # In common case, this will be O(1)\n        while True:\n            var = f\"{name}_{next(self.unique_var_id)}\"\n            if var not in existing:\n                self.code_options[\"co_varnames\"] += (var,)\n                return var\n\n    def update_co_names(self, name: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Ensure self.code_options.co_names contains name\"\"\"\n        if name not in self.code_options[\"co_names\"]:\n            self.code_options[\"co_names\"] += (name,)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def module_key_name(*names: Any) -> str:\n        # create a new unique name\n        name = \"_\".join(map(str, names))\n        # Strip _buffers[..]/_parameters[..]/_modules[..] names\n        name = re.sub(\n            r\"\\._(?:modules|parameters|buffers)\\[(['\\\"])([^'\\\"\\]]+)\\1\\]\", r\".\\2\", name\n        )\n        # Replace getattr(a, b) with a.b\n        name = re.sub(\n            r\"getattr\\(\\s*([^,]+?)\\s*,\\s*(['\\\"])([^'\\\"]+)\\2\\s*\\)\", r\"\\1.\\3\", name\n        )\n        # Strip the guard lookup L/G access\n        name = re.sub(r\"^[GL]\\['?(.*?)'?\\]$\", r\"\\1\", name)\n        # e.g. replace abc.xyz[123].qkv with abc.xyz_123.qkv\n        name = re.sub(r\"\\[(\\d+)\\]\", r\"_\\g<1>\", name)\n        # e.g. replace abc.xyz_123.qkv with abc_xyz_123_qkv\n        name = re.sub(r\"[^a-zA-Z0-9]\", \"_\", name)\n\n        if not name or not name[0].isalpha():\n            name = \"sub\" + name\n\n        return name\n\n    def register_static_attr_and_return_proxy(\n        self, attr_prefix: str, attr_value: Any\n    ) -> fx.Proxy:\n        # Check if the module already exists, if it does, return the already\n        # added proxy. This is important for executorch tests.\n        if isinstance(attr_value, torch.nn.Module):\n            for name, mod in self.nn_modules.items():\n                if mod is attr_value:\n                    proxy = self.create_proxy(\"get_attr\", name, (), {})\n                    return proxy\n\n        attr_name = get_unique_name_wrt(attr_prefix, self.nn_modules)\n        # TODO `nn_modules` has been historically overloaded to store a lot more\n        # than just nn module objects, fix that.\n        self.nn_modules[attr_name] = attr_value\n        proxy = self.create_proxy(\"get_attr\", attr_name, (), {})\n        set_example_value(proxy.node, attr_value)\n        return proxy\n\n    def register_attr_or_module(\n        self,\n        target: Union[torch.nn.Module, torch.Tensor, Any],\n        *names: Any,\n        **options: Any,\n    ) -> VariableTracker:\n        if is_dynamic_nn_module(target, self.export):\n            # Instead of returning UnspecializedNNModuleVariable, call\n            # VariableTracker.build so that it is tracked for mutation.\n            return VariableTracker.build(self.current_tx, target, **options)\n\n        options = dict(options)\n        assert \"source\" in options\n        source = options[\"source\"]\n        assert not isinstance(source, ParamBufferSource)\n\n        if isinstance(target, torch.Tensor):\n            tracer = self.current_tracer\n            if not self.is_root_tracer():\n                # For higher order ops, we don't want to insert the get_attr in\n                # innermost graph. Instead, we want to raise the params/buffers\n                # as inputs to the higher-order graph, and register them as\n                # get_attrs in the root tracer.\n\n                # Note that Dynamo will still call lift_tracked_freevar_to_input\n                # when these inputs are encountered for the inner graph. The\n                # only difference is what happens at the root tracer for\n                # nn.Parameters vs free inputs. The free inputs are registered\n                # as placeholders in the root graph, whereas the nn.Parameters\n                # are registered as get_attr nodes in the root graph.\n                tracer = self.root_tracer\n\n            def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\n                assert self.param_name_to_source is not None\n                self.param_name_to_source[module_key] = source\n\n                # Check if the attr has already been registered. This can happen\n                # when two different sources point to the same tensor.\n                assert self.root_tx is not None\n                if target in self.root_tx.output.side_effects:\n                    return self.root_tx.output.side_effects[target]\n\n                if get_static_address_type(target) == \"guarded\" and not isinstance(\n                    source, NumpyTensorSource\n                ):\n                    install_guard(source.make_guard(GuardBuilder.ID_MATCH))\n                elif not is_constant_source(source):\n                    install_guard(source.make_guard(GuardBuilder.TENSOR_MATCH))\n\n                vt = wrap_fx_proxy(\n                    self.root_tx,\n                    tracer.create_proxy(\"get_attr\", module_key, (), {}),\n                    example_value=target,\n                    **options,\n                )\n\n                # Track the object so to avoid duplicate registration in case of\n                # different sources pointing to the same tensor object.\n                vt = self.root_tx.output.side_effects.track_object_existing(target, vt)\n\n                assert \"tensor_dict\" not in vt.as_proxy().node.meta\n                # pyrefly: ignore [bad-argument-type]\n                vt.as_proxy().node.meta[\"tensor_dict\"] = _extract_tensor_dict(target)\n\n                return vt\n\n        elif isinstance(target, torch.nn.Module):\n            assert isinstance(target, torch.nn.Module)\n\n            if source:\n                install_guard(source.make_guard(GuardBuilder.NN_MODULE))\n\n                def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\n                    # pyrefly: ignore [bad-argument-type]\n                    return NNModuleVariable(type(target), module_key, target, **options)\n\n            else:\n                # This is Dynamo created graph module, e.g., graph module coming\n                # from higher order ops. NNModuleVariable tracker can't be\n                # sourceless, so let's return a unspecializedNNModule variable\n                # tracker.\n                def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\n                    # pyrefly: ignore[bad-argument-type]\n                    return variables.UnspecializedNNModuleVariable(target, **options)\n\n        elif isinstance(target, (torch.SymInt, torch.SymFloat)):\n            # HACKY CODE REGION BEGIN\n            # WE ARE PIGGYBACKING ON EXISTING INFRA TO REGISTER ATTRS\n            # This ultimately gets written to self.nn_modules, which is unfortunate\n            # Attrs that are tenors and symints and such need to be migrated to have their\n            # own storage\n            # alas, this is like this for now\n\n            def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\n                return SymNodeVariable.create(\n                    self.root_tx,\n                    self.create_proxy(\"get_attr\", module_key, (), {}),\n                    sym_num=target,\n                    **options,\n                )\n\n            # HACKY CODE REGION END\n        else:\n\n            def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\n                self.output.update_co_names(module_key)\n                self.global_scope[module_key] = target\n                return VariableTracker.build(\n                    self,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n                    target,\n                    ConstantSource(source_name=module_key),\n                )\n\n        for k, v in self.nn_modules.items():\n            if v is target:\n                # it already exists\n                return wrap_name(k)\n\n        name = OutputGraph.module_key_name(*names)\n        name = get_unique_name_wrt(name, self.nn_modules, self.global_scope)\n        self.nn_modules[name] = target\n        if isinstance(target, torch.nn.Module):\n\n            def register_leaf_name(leaf_name: str) -> None:\n                assert self.param_name_to_source is not None\n                new_source = self.get_chained_param_buffer_source(source, leaf_name)\n                new_name = f\"{name}.{leaf_name}\"\n                self.param_name_to_source[new_name] = new_source\n                if isinstance(source, LocalSource):\n                    self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn[\n                        OutputGraph.module_key_name(new_source.name)\n                    ] = leaf_name\n\n            # annoying, but there are cases when we do not have parameters\n            # see test_nn_moduledict_contains\n            if hasattr(target, \"_parameters\"):\n                for leaf_name, _ in target.named_parameters():\n                    register_leaf_name(leaf_name)\n            if hasattr(target, \"_buffers\"):\n                for leaf_name, _ in target.named_buffers():\n                    register_leaf_name(leaf_name)\n\n        return wrap_name(name)\n\n    def handle_aliases_for_stolen_lists(\n        self, tx: \"InstructionTranslatorBase\"\n    ) -> tuple[list[Instruction], dict[Source, Source]]:\n        # If list inputs are stolen, but still needed after the function call, create aliases to keep them alive\n        maybe_gm = self.local_scope.get(\"self\")\n        stolen_list_names = get_locals_to_steal(maybe_gm)\n        if not stolen_list_names:\n            return [], {}\n\n        alias_insts = []\n        needs_alias: dict[str, list[VariableTracker]] = {}\n\n        queue = [\n            *tx.stack,\n            *tx.symbolic_locals.values(),\n            *self.side_effects.store_attr_mutations.keys(),\n        ]\n\n        while queue:\n            x = queue.pop()\n            if isinstance(x, BaseListVariable):\n                assert isinstance(x.items, list)\n                queue += x.items\n                continue\n\n            if not (\n                (\n                    x not in self.side_effects.store_attr_mutations\n                    or isinstance(x.mutation_type, AttributeMutationExisting)\n                )\n                and isinstance(x.source, GetItemSource)\n                and isinstance(x.source.base, LocalSource)\n                and x.source.base.local_name in stolen_list_names\n            ):\n                continue\n\n            stolen_name = x.source.base.local_name\n            if stolen_name not in needs_alias:\n                needs_alias[stolen_name] = []\n            needs_alias[stolen_name].append(x)\n\n        # pyrefly: ignore [implicit-any]\n        visited = {}\n        overridden_sources: dict[Source, Source] = {}\n        for arg in self.graphargs:\n            if not (\n                isinstance(arg._example, list)\n                and isinstance(arg.source, LocalSource)\n                and arg.source.local_name in needs_alias\n            ):\n                continue\n\n            # arg is a list that will be cleared by the compiled function\n            list_name = arg.source.local_name\n            assert list_name in self.code_options[\"co_varnames\"]\n            for x in needs_alias[list_name]:\n                # Skip if already handled.\n                if x.source in overridden_sources:\n                    continue\n\n                # A small codegen optimization because we might have different\n                # VariableTrackers that share the same source.\n                assert x.source is not None\n                list_idx = x.source.index  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n                if list_idx not in visited:\n                    alias_name = self.new_var(\n                        f\"{list_name}_ref\"\n                    )  # self.new_var already adds unique id suffix\n\n                    visited[list_idx] = alias_name\n                    # bytecode of `alias_name = list_name[list_idx]`\n                    alias_insts.extend(\n                        [\n                            create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=list_name),\n                            create_load_const(list_idx),\n                            create_binary_subscr(),\n                            create_instruction(\"STORE_FAST\", argval=alias_name),\n                        ]\n                    )\n\n                # operate on alias, handled by suffix codegen\n                assert x.source is not None\n                old_source = x.source\n                overridden_sources[old_source] = LocalSource(visited[list_idx])\n\n        # NOTE: we need `overridden_sources` because (1) we want to codegen for\n        # these list items to use the new local source, but (2) we want to avoid\n        # updating `source` in place because that might break invariants in\n        # other parts of Dynamo like guards.\n        return alias_insts, overridden_sources\n\n    def _get_stack_values_to_restore(\n        self, tx: \"InstructionTranslatorBase\", stack_pops: int\n    ) -> tuple[list[VariableTracker], StackLocalsMetadata]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Gets the stack + locals values belonging to tx that need to be restored.\n\n        Also prunes dead tx locals and realizes all VTs in the tx's stack.\n\n        NullVariables in stack/locals will NOT be restored, unless they are the top `stack_pops`\n        elements of the stack - it is expected that the next instruction to run will pop the top\n        `stack_pops` elements of the stack, so we should codegen NULLs.\n\n        Returns:\n            - stack_values: stack and locals values that need to be restored\n            - meta: locations of NULLs and ContextWrappingVariables in the stack/locals\n                (ignores the top `stack_pops` values on the stack)\n        \"\"\"\n        tx.prune_dead_locals()\n\n        stack_values = []\n        meta = StackLocalsMetadata()\n\n        def ctx_exit_check(var: VariableTracker) -> None:\n            if type.__instancecheck__(variables.WithExitFunctionVariable, var):\n                raise AssertionError(\n                    \"Attempted to reconstruct WithExitFunctionVariable outside the stack\"\n                )\n\n        # realize any unrealized tensor VTs in case they\n        # need to be added to self.nn_modules as attributes\n        for i, value in enumerate(tx.stack):\n            # Allow lazy constants through for values being returned (top of stack)\n            allow_lazy_constant = len(tx.stack) - i <= stack_pops\n            variables.LazyVariableTracker.realize_all(\n                value, allow_lazy_constant=allow_lazy_constant\n            )\n            # Do not allow non-stack WithExitFunctionVariable reconstructions\n            if not isinstance(value, variables.WithExitFunctionVariable):\n                VariableTracker.visit(ctx_exit_check, value)\n            # ignore top `stack_pops` values on the stack\n            if allow_lazy_constant:\n                stack_values.append(value)\n                continue\n            if isinstance(value, NullVariable):\n                meta.stack_null_idxes.append(i)\n            else:\n                stack_values.append(value)\n            if isinstance(value, ContextWrappingVariable):\n                target_values = (\n                    () if value.target_values is None else tuple(value.target_values)\n                )\n                # NOTE: track index in stack after NULLs have been removed\n                meta.stack_ctx_args.append((len(stack_values) - 1, target_values))\n                meta.stack_ctx_idxes_orig.append(i)\n\n        meta.num_stack = len(stack_values)\n\n        cell_and_freevars = set(tx.cellvars() + tx.freevars())\n\n        # NB: Typically (i.e., for graph compile from RETURN_VALUE),\n        # symbolic_locals will be empty at this point, as prune_dead_locals\n        # will clear out all of symbolic_locals because RETURN_VALUE is the\n        # last instruction and no more locals are used.  The fanciness here\n        # is only needed for partial graphs.\n        # NOTE: All cell and free variables are represented as CellVariable,\n        # so checks for NULLs and context managers in the case of codegen'ing resume\n        # functions will not be performed on them. This is expected behavior.\n        for k, v in tx.symbolic_locals.items():\n            # Do not reconstruct WithExitFunctionVariable!\n            VariableTracker.visit(ctx_exit_check, v)\n            # Note! this explicitly uses .local_name for matching\n            # Failure to do so will cause spurious registrations in val_to_names.\n            # This will in turn result in spurious variables showing up in the graph.\n            # This was very tricky to debug. For an example, dump the graph at call_user_compiler\n            # while running test_subgraphs.py\n            # Do not include top-frame unmodified locals here - otherwise, the compiled graph may\n            # erroneously include them as part of the return.
